name: Elijah Pedigo Hardy
source: Habersham Co Cem, Ga Rosters, FAG, NPS+
notes: FAG #17479095. Enlisted in Habersham Co, Ga. Okla. He enlisted as a Private on March 4, 1862 into Company A of the 52nd Regiment, GA Infantry, Army of Tennessee, CSA "For a period of three years." In December of 1862, he was elected Third Lieutenant and may have been transferred to Company A of the 32nd Infantry as he is shown as serving in that unit also. He was captured at Baker's Creek, MS on May 16, 1863. He served until when he was captured at Adairsville, GA on May 18, 1864. He was exchanged on March, 1865. Widow's Pension. Father: John Hardy (1809Ã¢â¬â1889)

name: Lewis C Tennent Dr
cemetery_name: North McAlester Cemetery (AKA: North Town Cemetery)
source: UCV Organization of Camps 1896 & Find A Grave
notes: Member of the UCV. In 1896, Louis C. Tennent of McAlester, I.T., was named Adjutant General and Chief of Staff of the Indian Territory Division of the UCV.  Per comments on Find A Grave Memorial  of Confederate Army POW in Pennsylvania. Born in Edgefield County, South Carolina. The son of Gilbert & Carolyn (Groves) Tennent. He ran off at the young age of 16 to join the service during the Civil War. Married Emma H. McDuff Nov. 25, 1872. She was a member of the Choctaw tribe.

name: Reuben M Folsom
notes: FAG #35415698. Enlisted: Campbell Co, GA. Wounded at Sharpsburg, MD September 17, 1862. Captured at Gettysburg, PA July 4, 1863. Paroled at Point Lookout Prison, MD for exchange February 18, 1865. No later record. [Record updated 8/18/2022, G. Daniell, Ga Div Graves Proj, SCV]

name: James Henry Peck
source: Lumpkin Co Cem; Ga Rosters; FAG+

Deserted July or August 1863 . Joined from desertion September 26 1863  and placed under arrest. Absent sick August 31 1864 . Father: Adam Peck (1791â1866)

name: Wilkes Wylie Crockett
notes: FAG #100518880. Enlisted: Decatur, DeKalb Co, GA. Private June 24, 1861. Elected Jr. 2d Lieutenant November 9, 1862. Absent without leave February 8-28, 1865. No later record. Pension App in DeKalb Co, GA. States that he was present at surrender at Appomattox, VA Apr 9, 1865 in the Pension App. [Record updated 7/3/2023, G. Daniell, Ga Div Graves Proj, SCV]

name: Thomas Leander Ashworth
source: Fannin Cems; Ga Rosters; NPS; FAG+
notes: FAG #31862542. Enlisted in Fannin Co GA. Enlisted in Co C Inf Battn Smith's Legion Ga Vols. May 14 1862. Appointed 4th Sergeant. Transferred to Co B 65th Regiment Ga Inf March 1863. On 30 days detached service in Fannin Co Ga to arrest deserters August 19 1863. Sick in hospital August 31 1864. Father: Jasper Joshua Ashworth (1816-1881)
